item.title: OpenAI updates its Agents SDK to help enterprises build safer, more capable agents
item.dc:creator: Lucas Ropek
item.description: OpenAI has expanded the capabilities of its agent-building toolkit, as agentic AI continues to grow in popularity.

item.title: Hightouch reaches $100M ARR fueled by marketing tools powered by AI
item.dc:creator: Marina Temkin
item.description: The startup says it grew its ARR by $70 million in just 20 months after launching an AI agent platform for marketers.

item.title: LinkedIn data shows AI isn’t to blame for hiring decline… yet
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: LinkedIn says hiring is down 20% since 2022, but blames higher interest rates — not AI — for the slowdown.

item.title: AI learning app Gizmo levels up with 13M users and a $22M investment
item.dc:creator: Lauren Forristal
item.description: Gizmo, an AI-powered learning platform, has attracted more than 13 million users and just secured $22 million in Series A funding.

item.title: Can AI judge journalism? A Thiel-backed startup says yes, even if it risks chilling whistleblowers
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Bellan
item.description: Objection, a Thiel-backed startup, aims to use AI to judge journalism, letting users pay to challenge stories. Critics warn it could chill whistleblowers and reshape how media accountability works.

item.title: Google rolls out a native Gemini app for Mac
item.dc:creator: Aisha Malik
item.description: You can share anything on their screen with Gemini to get help with what they're looking at in the moment, including local files.

item.title: The musician-turned-biotech-founder waiting to fundraise
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Bellan, Theresa Loconsolo
item.description: When Grammy-nominated singer-songwriter Aloe Blacc got COVID despite being vaccinated and boosted, he tried to fund research for a better solution. What he quickly found out? You can’t just write a check in biotech. Regulators require a commercialization plan, and philanthropy doesn’t move science through clinical trials or get you a license on university IP. Now, he’s bootstrapping a cancer drug platform targeting pancreatic cancer, a disease that […]

item.title: India’s vibe-coding startup Emergent enters OpenClaw-like AI agent space
item.dc:creator: Jagmeet Singh
item.description: Emergent's Wingman lets users manage and automate tasks through chat on platforms like WhatsApp and Telegram.

item.title: After sale of its shoe business, Allbirds pivots to AI
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: Allbirds is ditching wool sneakers for AI servers, rebranding as NewBird AI after locking in a $50M convertible financing facility.

item.title: Reid Hoffman weighs in on the ‘tokenmaxxing’ debate
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: Reid Hoffman says tracking AI token use can gauge adoption, but cautions it should be paired with context and not treated as a direct productivity metric.

item.title: Adobe’s new Firefly AI assistant can use Creative Cloud apps to complete tasks
item.dc:creator: Ivan Mehta
item.description: Adobe says the assistant can work across apps like Firefly, Photoshop, Premiere, Lightroom, Express, Illustrator and its other apps to do tasks for you.

item.title: Gitar, a startup that uses agents to secure code, emerges from stealth with $9 million
item.dc:creator: Lucas Ropek
item.description: The company uses AI to review code that, more often than not these days, has also been generated by AI.

item.title: This startup is betting tokenmaxxing will create the next compute giant
item.dc:creator: Tim Fernholz
item.description: Parasail raised $32 million in a Series A, signaling a fractured future of models and compute.

item.title: Anthropic’s rise is giving some OpenAI investors second thoughts
item.dc:creator: Connie Loizos
item.description: One investor who has backed both companies told the FT that justifying OpenAI's recent round required assuming an IPO valuation of $1.2 trillion or more — making Anthropic's current $380 billion valuation look like the relative bargain.

item.title: Anthropic co-founder confirms the company briefed the Trump administration on Mythos
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: In an interview at the Semafor World Economy summit this week, Anthropic co-founder Jack Clark explained why the company was still engaged with the U.S. government while simultaneously suing them.

item.title: Max Hodak’s Science Corp. is preparing to place its first sensor in a human brain
item.dc:creator: Tim Fernholz
item.description: The device could help address multiple neurological conditions if it proves successful. One early use could be delivering gentle electrical stimulation to damaged brain or spinal cord cells to encourage healing.

item.title: Google adds AI Skills to Chrome to help you save favorite workflows
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: Google is adding “Skills” to Chrome, letting users save and reuse AI prompts across websites. The feature builds on Gemini’s browser integration.

item.title: In just a couple weeks, StrictlyVC San Francisco brings leaders from TDK Ventures, Replit, and more together
item.dc:creator: TechCrunch Events
item.description:
The first StrictlyVC of the year will be coming to San Francisco before you know it.
There are still a few tickets available to join us and our stacked speaker lineup April 30, so register today!

item.title: Google brings its Gemini Personal Intelligence feature to India
item.dc:creator: Ivan Mehta
item.description: The feature lets users connect Google accounts like Gmail and Photos to get personalized answers.
